title: tridophyllum rivale
parts: Tridophyllum rivale has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds. The roots of Tridophyllum rivale are rich in alkaloids, including tridophyllumine A and B, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids, like quercetin and kaempferol, that have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer activities. Additionally, the flowers of Tridophyllum rivale contain glycosides, including tridophyllumoside, which have been reported to have sedative and antispasmodic effects.
side_effects: Tridophyllum rivale can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high content of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its toxic alkaloids can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as cardiovascular problems, such as arrhythmias and hypertension. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Tridophyllum rivale can cause neurological symptoms, such as dizziness, confusion, and even seizures. It is essential to consult with a medical professional or a qualified herbalist before using Tridophyllum rivale or any other plant for medicinal purposes.
